nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; generally found in sheltered lagoons and seaward reef areas, typically taking shelter in rockwork, among branching corals, sea-urchin spines, or anemones during the day and emerging to forage at night. Reported depth range 1–50 m, most commonly 1–15 m.

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied diet of small meaty foods such as mysid shrimp, brine shrimp, finely chopped marine flesh, frozen crustaceans, and vitamin-enriched prepared foods. Pellet and flake foods are commonly accepted once settled, though some specimens initially refuse dry foods. Multiple short feedings per day tend to be better than a single large meal, and evening feeding has been reported to improve acceptance given the species' nocturnal foraging tendency.

summary: A small, peaceful Indo-Pacific cardinalfish with electric-blue body stripes on a yellow ground. Generally beginner-friendly, reef-safe, and best kept as a small shoal in a well-aquascaped reef with plenty of rockwork retreats.
careNotes:
The Yellowstriped Cardinalfish is a small, slender Indo-Pacific reef fish prized for its bright yellow body crossed by six iridescent blue-to-purple horizontal stripes. It is one of the more commonly imported small cardinalfishes and is generally considered an easy entry-level marine species, provided its shoaling temperament and preference for shaded rockwork are accommodated.

In the wild it is associated with sheltered lagoons and seaward reefs and is typically encountered in small aggregations sheltering among branching corals, rockwork, sea-urchin spines, or anemones during the day. In aquaria it tends to be most settled when given ample live rock with caves and overhangs in which to retreat, and when kept in a small group of at least 5–7 specimens introduced simultaneously. Sources consistently report that conspecifics establish a loose hierarchy without overt aggression when housed together, in contrast to several other cardinalfishes that fight as adults.

Water parameters should sit in the typical tropical-marine reef range: temperature roughly 24–28°C, pH 8.1–8.4, specific gravity 1.020–1.025, and alkalinity around 8–12 dKH. The species is reported as reef-safe and has not been documented to nip corals or sessile invertebrates; small ornamental shrimp are generally tolerated, though as with most marine carnivores, very small newly added shrimp may occasionally be eaten.

The species is largely nocturnal in its feeding habits — in nature it leaves shelter at dusk to forage on zooplankton and small crustaceans. In captivity it tends to adapt to daytime feeding, but evening feeding and dim/shaded refuges generally help newly added specimens settle and feed reliably.

Cross-source notes: LiveAquaria gave a maximum adult size of 2.5 in (≈6.4 cm) and a minimum tank size of 10 gallons (≈38 L); FishBase gives 8.0 cm TL maximum, and AquaticCommunity gives 100 L (26 gal) as the minimum. The values written here (6–8 cm; 100 L) average across sources and bias toward the larger tank to accommodate the species' shoaling behaviour and need for rockwork — 38 L is generally too small for a meaningful group. Temperature ranges also differed (FishBase 25–29.3°C wild; aquarium-hobby sources 22.2–25.6°C); the value written (24–28°C) is a typical reef-tropical compromise.
breedingNotes: The Yellowstriped Cardinalfish is a paternal mouthbrooder, like other apogonids. After distinct pair courtship and spawning, the male carries the egg mass in his buccal cavity for roughly 1–3 weeks and largely ceases feeding during this period; a visibly swollen jaw is the most obvious sign of brooding. Spawning has been reported in well-established reef systems with cohesive small groups, though dedicated breeding setups generally improve fry survival as larvae are pelagic and vulnerable to predation in community tanks.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source — gave scientific name, care level (easy), temperament (peaceful), minimum tank size (10 gal), reef-safe status, diet, and the no-aggression-when-grouped behaviour.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Apogonidae, Kurtiformes), confirmed max size 8.0 cm TL (common 6.0 cm SL), wild temperature range 25–29.3°C, Indo-Pacific distribution, mouthbrooder reproduction, and IUCN Least Concern status.

additional.name: AquaticCommunity
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (7 cm), minimum tank size (100 L / 26 gal), care level (hardy), temperament (peaceful), reef-safe (always), diet (krill/mysis/artemia/cyclops), and confirmed nocturnal habits + need for rock shelter.

additional.notes: Cross-checked tank size, peaceful temperament, shoaling preference, and reef-safe status. Returned via WebSearch summary.

additional.name: Quality Marine / general aquarium-hobby search
additional.notes: Cross-checked salinity (1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), kH (8–12) and shoaling group size (5–7 individuals introduced simultaneously). Also confirmed the 'Orange-lined Cardinalfish' trade name.

fish.breedingNotes: Paternal mouthbrooder. Has spawned in captivity in cohesive small shoals; males carry egg mass for ~1–3 weeks and stop feeding during brooding. Fry survival in community tanks tends to be low due to predation.
